title: Commit creation
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: Extend gitops (or new internal/commit package): CommitTask(ctx, sup, worktreePath, message) asserts the worktree's current branch is not the repository's default branch (defense-in-depth on top of WorktreeManager already only creating task branches, per §15.4 'no direct edits on default branch'), requires a passed diffcheck.Report (re-running the secret scan internally as a hard gate, not advisory), captures base SHA (recorded at worktree creation) and resulting commit SHA after 'git add -A && git commit -m', and records both as evidence (§17.1 'commit' evidence type). MCP tool commit_task.
notes: Per §15.4 and §17.2 (task.yaml/summary.yaml need the commit reference). Security-sensitive (mutates git history) - building myself, tightly coupled to bap.7's diff checker.
close_reason: gitops.WorktreeManager.CommitTask requires a passed diff-check result and a punakawan/-prefixed task branch (defense-in-depth per §15.4's 'no direct edits on default branch') before staging and committing, then records base and resulting commit SHAs. Worktree.BaseSHA is now captured at creation time via a new Inspector.HeadSHA method. Exposed as MCP tool commit_task. 3/3 new gitops tests pass, plus the full MCP lifecycle test exercises a real commit end-to-end.

title: OpenAPI compatibility check
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: New internal/openapicheck package wrapping a real Go OpenAPI-diff library (verify current, real API via Context7 before coding - do not guess; oasdiff is the plan's named tool per §13.4/§48). Check(ctx, baseSpecPath, headSpecPath) parses both specs, diffs operations, classifies breaking vs non-breaking changes, and writes api-diff.json into the evidence bundle. MCP tool check_openapi_compatibility.
notes: Per §13.4 ('Use parser libraries plus oasdiff... detect breaking changes, generate compatibility evidence') and §17.1's 'api diff' evidence type. Delegated: self-contained, no repository mutation. Must ground the actual library API in real docs (Context7), not assumed syntax.
close_reason: internal/openapicheck.Check diffs two OpenAPI specs via the real oasdiff library (github.com/oasdiff/oasdiff/diff + checker, API confirmed via Context7 plus direct go doc/source reading for the aggregator checker.CheckBackwardCompatibility, which was not documented by Context7 alone) and classifies breaking vs non-breaking changes, writing api-diff.json evidence (§13.4, §17.2). Exposed as MCP tool check_openapi_compatibility. 5/5 tests pass against real fixture specs (no-change, compatible addition, breaking required-param removal).

title: Finding severity model

title: Unit and integration test execution
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: New internal/testrun package: Run(ctx, sup, dir, commands []Command) executes each caller-specified command via the existing tools.Supervisor (which already enforces §11.4's working-dir allowlist/env allowlist/timeout/output-size limits), captures exit code/stdout/stderr per command, and writes a tests.json-shaped report into the evidence bundle (§17.2). MCP tool run_tests.
notes: Per §11.3 execution loop steps 'compile'/'run targeted tests' and §17.1 'test report' evidence type. Go does not know project-specific test commands beyond what the caller (Petruk/external role) supplies - this is a thin, policy-bounded executor, not a test framework. Delegated: builds on existing Supervisor, no new mutation surface.
close_reason: internal/testrun.Run executes caller-specified commands via the existing tools.Supervisor (no reimplementation of its safety controls), continuing past a failing command so all results are captured, and writes tests.json evidence (§17.2). Exposed as MCP tool run_tests. 4/4 tests pass against real subprocesses.

title: Diff checker
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: New internal/diffcheck package: Check(ctx, sup, worktreePath, pol) runs 'git diff --name-only' and 'git diff' via Supervisor, checks each changed file against policy.AllowsFilesystemWrite, scans diff content with a documented heuristic secret-pattern set (AWS key patterns, 'password=', private-key headers, etc - explicitly not a full secret-scanning product), writes diff.patch into the evidence bundle (§17.2), and returns a Report{Allowed, Violations, ChangedFiles}. MCP tool check_diff.
notes: Per §15.4 'block commits containing detected secrets' and 'verify repository root before writing'. Security-sensitive gate that commit_task (bap.10) depends on - building myself.
close_reason: internal/diffcheck.Check stages a worktree's pending changes (git add -A), checks each changed file against policy and a documented heuristic secret-pattern set (AWS keys, PEM headers, password/secret-key assignments), and writes diff.patch evidence (§17.2). Exposed as MCP tool check_diff. 4/4 tests pass: clean change allowed, policy-denied path blocked, AWS-key-shaped secret blocked, new+deleted files both captured.

title: File editing abstraction

design: New internal/fileops package: WriteFile(pol, worktreeRoot, relPath, content) resolves relPath against worktreeRoot with path-traversal rejection (must stay within worktreeRoot after Clean+Abs, per §15.4 'prevent path traversal'), checks policy.AllowsFilesystemWrite(relPath) (reusing existing internal/policy), then writes (creating parent dirs). close_reason: internal/fileops.WriteFile checks policy.AllowsFilesystemWrite and rejects path traversal (relative-clean-based, not clamping, so escapes are rejected rather than silently redirected) before writing within a task's worktree. title: Worktree executor
description: Deliverable of M6: Plan-to-Beads and Petruk execution. Source: punakawan-go-typescript-detailed-plan.md §22.
design: New internal/execution package: StartTaskExecution wraps gitops.WorktreeManager.RequestApproval/Approve-check/Create + evidence.NewBundle + evidence.OpenJournal (§11.1 steps 1-4), returning a Session{Worktree, Bundle, Journal}. FinishTaskExecution appends the final journal event and calls WorktreeManager.Remove (step 10). Steps 5-9 (load knowledge/edit/checks/evidence/commit) are separate MCP tools (build_task_context, write_file/bulk_create_files, run_tests, check_diff, commit_task) operating against Session.Worktree.Path - Punakawan never calls an LLM itself (§28), so the external role does the actual edit; Go only owns the orchestration and side effects around it. MCP tools: start_task_execution, finish_task_execution.
notes: Per §11.1's 10-step loop and §11.3. I am building this myself: it is the architecturally central orchestration all other M6 tools plug into, and touches git worktree lifecycle (already security-sensitive per gitops.WorktreeManager's existing approval gate).
close_reason: internal/execution.StartTaskExecution/FinishTaskExecution orchestrate §11.1 steps 1-4 and 10 (worktree creation via gitops.WorktreeManager, evidence bundle/journal open, final status event, worktree removal). Exposed as MCP tools start_task_execution/finish_task_execution, reconstructing session state across the two stateless calls from deterministic paths (gitops.WorktreePath, evidence.OpenJournal). 3/3 unit tests plus a full MCP wire-protocol lifecycle test (TestTaskExecutionLifecycle: start -> write_file -> check_diff -> commit_task -> finish) all pass.

title: Add delete_knowledge and reset_project_knowledge MCP tools
description: User asked (2026-07-23) for tools so an agent can remove stale knowledge from search_knowledge results and avoid dirty context: delete_knowledge (bulk delete by explicit ids) and reset_project_knowledge (scope-filtered bulk wipe, defaults to dry-run, requires confirm=true to delete). Also fixed search.Rebuild to prune index entries for records deleted from the store, and wired a delete knowledge-event to match Put/Supersede's existing audit trail.
design: Implemented directly in internal/mcpserver/tools_knowledgedelete.go, internal/search/index.go (Rebuild diff-and-prune), internal/knowledge/service.go+events.go (delete event).
acceptance_criteria: delete_knowledge and reset_project_knowledge tools exist, are tested, and search.Rebuild no longer leaves stale index entries after a delete.
close_reason: Shipped in commit 62cde66: delete_knowledge, reset_project_knowledge (dry-run by default, confirm=true to delete), search.Rebuild now prunes stale index entries on delete, and Store.Delete emits a delete knowledge-event. Full test suite green (370 tests, 32 packages).

title: Wire policy.yaml Git section into GitExecutionPolicy's repository-policy layer
description: push_task_branch/create_pr currently use gitops.DefaultExecutionPolicy (allow-everything) as the repository-policy input to MergeExecutionPolicy, since policy.yaml has no Git section yet. Add a policy.GitPolicy (mirroring ApprovalsPolicy) with allow_push/allow_pull_request_creation/etc, load it in internal/app.Load, and pass it through instead of the hardcoded default.

title: Wire the missing-context-request round trip
description: A subagent's MissingContextRequest (plan section 6.4) must return to Semar, which decides to search/add-to-capsule-revision/reject/ask-the-user. No such round trip exists today - subagents currently just call punakawan's data tools directly with no capsule-scoped request/response cycle.
close_reason: Shipped internal/contextrequest (fold-latest-per-id JSONL store) plus submit_missing_context_request, list_missing_context_requests, and resolve_missing_context_request MCP tools. Punakawan persists the request/resolution but never chooses between §6.4's four Semar decisions - resolve_missing_context_request just validates added_to_revision names a real capsule (from a prior request_capsule call) and records whichever choice the calling agent made.

title: Implement Semar's knowledge-retrieval-to-capsule pipeline
description: Extract identifiers from the task, run exact+BM25 search, expand one-hop relations, rerank by scope, select within token budget, and build a ContextCapsule recording why each item was included (plan section 15). Depends on AEP-M1 (capsules) and AEP-M6 (search).
notes: Per user decision 2026-07-23: fold stale-knowledge reconciliation into this pipeline. internal/reconcile.CheckSourceStale exists (built in an earlier milestone) but nothing calls it - wire it in here so records get re-checked against their external source right before Semar retrieves them for a capsule, rather than building a separate standalone reconcile tool/scheduler.
close_reason: capsule.BuildFromRetrieval implements the pipeline: search.Search (exact-id+BM25F+fuzzy+one-hop relations, already built in M6), role-based filtering, token-budget selection, and reason-annotated relevant_knowledge. Wired into request_capsule via an optional retrieval_query. Stale-knowledge reconciliation folded in as a lazily-resolved, best-effort ReconcileGate per the 2026-07-23 user decision noted on this issue - no separate standalone reconcile tool was built.

title: Implement knowledge.search MCP tool with ranking and explain-match
description: Wire tokenizer + exact-id detection + alias matching + BM25F + fuzzy fallback + scope/trust/relation-expansion bonuses (plan sections 11.2, 11.9-11.13) into a single search tool that returns match.kind/fields/terms for explainability.
close_reason: Shipped search_knowledge MCP tool: full §11.2 pipeline (BM25F first, fuzzy fallback only on zero hits) plus §11.10's exact-identifier/alias/scope/trust ranking bonuses, §11.9's one-hop relation expansion, and a per-result Explanation for §11.13's explain-match, all returned via match.kind/fields/terms. The tool resyncs the disposable Bleve index (an idempotent full re-upsert via Rebuild) before every query so results are never stale, since no separate refresh_index tool exists yet in this milestone's scope.

title: Implement exact-identifier detection
description: Recognize CVE/GHSA/Sonar-rule/Jira-key/BD-task-id/git-commit-hash/Maven-coordinate/npm-package/PR-number patterns and rank exact identifier matches above every BM25 result (plan section 11.3).
close_reason: internal/search.DetectIdentifiers recognizes CVE/GHSA/Sonar-rule/Jira-key/BD-task-id/PR-number/git-hash/version-string/Maven-coordinate/npm-package/Go-module/file-path/API-route/Java-class-or-method/symbol patterns, ordered most-specific-first so e.g. a Sonar rule (java:S3776) is not mistaken for a generic Maven coordinate. Wired into scoreResult as the +100 exact-identifier bonus that outranks every BM25 result per §11.10.

title: Implement technical tokenizer
description: camelCase/PascalCase/snake_case/kebab-case/dotted-package/colon-coordinate splitting that preserves the original token (plan section 11.6). No aggressive stemming of technical identifiers.
close_reason: internal/search.Tokenize implements the technical tokenizer: splits camelCase/PascalCase/snake_case/kebab-case/dotted-package/colon-coordinate/slash-path tokens while always preserving the original and lowercased whole token, and never splits digits off adjoining letters (java8time stays whole) or explodes acronym runs (URLParser -> URL, Parser not U/R/L/Parser).

title: Integrate Bleve and define BM25F field mappings
description: No search library exists today (confirmed: go.mod has no Bleve/BM25 dependency). Add github.com/blevesearch/bleve and index knowledge_records on title/summary/content/aliases/tags/paths/symbols/identifiers per plan section 11.4-11.5's field weights.
close_reason: Added github.com/blevesearch/bleve/v2, a custom punakawan_technical analyzer backed directly by Tokenize, and internal/search.Index (Open/Close/IndexRecord/DeleteRecord/Rebuild) over §11.4's document model (title/summary/content/aliases/tags/paths/symbols/identifiers, plus type/project/repository/module/trustLevel as keyword fields). §11.5's field weights (identifiers 12.0 down to content 1.0) are applied as per-field query boosts in search.go's BM25F DisjunctionQuery, since Bleve has no static per-field weight on the mapping itself. protocol.KnowledgeRecord gained summary/content/aliases/tags/scope fields to give the document model something to index.

title: Add YAML portable export/import of the knowledge store
description: Plan section 10.1/10.2 calls for a portable.yaml human-readable export that can reconstruct essential knowledge without Dolt. Implement export (Store -> YAML) and import (YAML -> Store) round-trip.
close_reason: Added Store.ExportYAML/ImportYAML (PortableExport{Records}) alongside the existing JSONL Export/Import, giving §10.1's portable.yaml a full round trip through the same Put-based provenance validation as every other write path.

title: Add JSONL knowledge-event export
description: Append-only knowledge-events.jsonl (plan section 10.2) distinct from the existing approvals.jsonl/runs/<run>/events.jsonl - emit an event on every Put/Supersede for external tailing/audit.
close_reason: Added .punakawan/events/knowledge-events.jsonl (§10.1/§10.2): Store now emits a put event on every Put and an additional supersede event on every Supersede, readable back via Store.Events() for external tailing/audit.

title: Add supersededBy field and Supersede API to knowledge records
description: protocol.KnowledgeRecord has no supersession trail today - Put() overwrites in place. Add a supersededBy reference and a knowledge.Store.Supersede(id, supersededBy string) method that marks a record superseded without deleting it (plan section 10.4/12.8).
close_reason: Added superseded_by to protocol.KnowledgeRecord (knowledge.schema.json §10.4) and Store.Supersede(id, supersededBy): marks a record superseded in place via the existing Put path rather than deleting it.

title: Add retry/conflict-detection queue for outbound Jira sync failures
description: internal/adapters.Gate today either succeeds or hard-errors on an adapter write; there is no queued retry or conflict-detection for a Jira sync failure (plan section 9.4/Phase 5). Design a minimal outbound sync queue that records a failed write for retry without blocking BD-side task completion.
close_reason: Added internal/syncqueue.Queue (append-only JSONL, fold-latest-per-id like approvals.Store) and wired it into adapters.Gate.Call/Registry: a write that fails after passing approval is recorded with params, attempt count, and conflict detection against other pending entries for the same adapter/op/issue. New list_jira_sync_queue and retry_jira_sync_entry MCP tools find and replay queued failures. Nothing here blocks or is called by commit_task/finish_task_execution.

title: Add structured task-to-evidence reference on TaskContract
description: Evidence is currently linked to a task only by the .punakawan/evidence/<run>/<task>/ directory-naming convention (internal/evidence/bundle.go), not a struct field. Add an EvidenceReference list (or similar) so a TaskContract/GraphResult can enumerate its evidence without relying on path convention alone.
close_reason: Added internal/evidence.Ledger (append-only JSONL of protocol.EvidenceRecord) and RecordArtifact, wired into check_diff/run_tests/check_openapi_compatibility right after each writes its bundle file. New list_task_evidence MCP tool enumerates a task's recorded evidence structurally instead of by file-naming convention.

title: AEP-M7: Full Semar orchestration over capsules and search
description: Architecture enhancement plan Milestone 7. Depends on AEP-M1 (context capsules) and AEP-M6 (search) landing first - this milestone is Semar's knowledge-retrieval-to-capsule pipeline and the missing-context-request round trip, neither of which can exist before their dependencies. Today the connected MCP client's own model plays every role directly against punakawan's data tools; there is no punakawan-side dispatch of Gareng/Petruk/Bagong as separately constrained invocations.
close_reason: Both AEP-M7 children shipped: Semar's knowledge-retrieval-to-capsule pipeline (m2y, via capsule.BuildFromRetrieval + request_capsule's retrieval_query) and the missing-context-request round trip (uje, via internal/contextrequest + submit/list/resolve_missing_context_request). Semar now has both halves of §6: build a capsule with automatically-retrieved, budget-limited, reason-annotated knowledge, and a way for a subagent to ask for more without searching broadly itself.

title: AEP-M6: Local BM25 knowledge search
description: Architecture enhancement plan Milestone 6. Current state: does not exist at all. go.mod has no search/indexing library (no Bleve; confirmed by grep). internal/knowledge.Store.Get is exact-id lookup only; Store.Related is reverse-relation lookup only, not general search - it is used solely by taskcontext/dossier to populate specific fields, not exposed as a search capability. No tokenizer, no alias matching, no fuzzy fallback, no exact-identifier (CVE/GHSA/Sonar-rule/Jira-key) detection exists anywhere in the codebase.
close_reason: All four AEP-M6 children shipped in internal/search: technical tokenizer (44o), exact-identifier detection (69a), Bleve BM25F integration with §11.5 field weights (6hi), and the search_knowledge MCP tool with full ranking + explain-match (s1z). Local, embeddings-free, no external model calls, per §11.1's search goals. Unblocks AEP-M7 (punokawan-ovm).

title: AEP-M5: Durable knowledge core enhancements
description: Architecture enhancement plan Milestone 5. Current state: internal/knowledge already has Dolt-backed CRUD (Put upserts as revision), relations (Relations/Related), and content-hash staleness detection (internal/reconcile, knowledge.CheckStale/ContentHash). Gaps vs the plan: no supersededBy field or Supersede(id) API on protocol.KnowledgeRecord (records are overwritten in place by Put, with no explicit supersession trail); no JSONL knowledge-event export beyond approvals.jsonl/runs/<run>/events.jsonl; no YAML portable export/import of the knowledge store.
close_reason: All three AEP-M5 children shipped: supersededBy field + Supersede API (pkv), knowledge-events.jsonl audit trail (452), portable YAML export/import (ei9). Durable knowledge core now covers all four §10.1 storage layers (Dolt canonical, JSONL events, YAML portable, BM25 index still pending in AEP-M6).

title: Elicitation fallback error is raw zod JSON instead of a clean punakawan message
description: When an MCP client doesn't support form elicitation, the approval fallback surfaces a raw zod validation error blob rather than a formatted punakawan message with the Approve/Deny choices spelled out in plain text. It works but requires the caller to already know what the raw error means.
acceptance_criteria: The non-elicitation fallback path renders a plain-text message naming the pending approval id and the Approve/Deny CLI commands to run, with no raw zod/schema JSON in the surfaced text.
close_reason: Any elicitation error now falls back to the same clean ACTION REQUIRED message; removed the dead unsupported-substring allowlist that let unmatched errors (e.g. a client-side zod validation error) leak through raw

title: Improve error messages for one-off adapter writes outside the full task-graph pipeline
description: Real-world usage (planning/reviewing Jira ticket TRF-21481 via share-hub) showed that calling call_adapter_operation/update_jira_task_progress directly, without first running the full create_workflow_run -> submit_task_graph -> claim_ready_task -> plan/review pipeline, always fails with "not approved for run" - by design (RequestApproval only creates a pending record; a human must grant it via `punakawan approvals approve`, never another MCP tool). This is correct per the human-in-the-loop model but was not obvious in the moment: the error message does not point at the actual recovery path (punakawan approvals list/approve).
acceptance_criteria: The "not approved for run" error from adapters.Gate.Call (internal/adapters/gate.go) mentions the punakawan approvals CLI as the way to grant it. Consider also documenting the two supported usage patterns (full pipeline vs. one-off write + manual approval) somewhere a caller would find them (README or a tool description).
notes: Found while testing the real MCP flow against TRF-21481 in the share-hub repo. Not a bug in the gate itself - the gate behaved correctly - just a discoverability gap.
close_reason: Gate and elicitation fallback errors now include the exact punakawan approvals approve command and approval id. Server instructions, MCP tool descriptions, installer output, and README document inline approval and CLI fallback.
